lyrics:
It's the "Wanting" we desire
Not the thing…

There's a hunger in my bones that nobody could appease
Not a touch, not a taste, not a thousand offered keys
It's the pull of some horizon where my name is still unknown
Where the self I haven't met yet waits to claim me as its own

I don't chase the fleeting pleasures
That dissolve when night is through
I am burning for becoming
For the "me" I can't yet view

Desire is the fire that remakes me
Every time I shed my skin
Not a craving, but a calling
A beginning tucked within
I don't want what I can hold
So much as who I might become
Desire is the engine
Not the sum

Every version I've outgrown is a ghost I kiss goodbye
Thanking it for all the ways it taught me how to die
I am not the past reflections, nor the mirrors yet to be
I am just the restless motion toward a wider kind of me

I won't let my wanting shrink me
Into greed or little wars
I will let it be a compass
Pointing past my current shores

Desire is the fire that remakes me
Not a hunger born of lack
It's the current in the river
Pulling forward, never back
I don't want the world to feed me
I want room enough to grow
Desire is the question
Not the "no."

Let every wish that chains me
Fall away, unnamed
Let every wish that wakes me
Be the one I claim

Desire is the fire that remakes me
As I shed what I outgrow
If I chase what dulls my spirit
Then I'm choosing not to know
I will want with open eyes
Let the wanting make me new
Desire is the doorway
I walk through
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This is a high-octane heavy metal track that projects an atmosphere of defiant ambition through its dense, modern texture. The energy is consistently high, creating a driving, anthemic feel with powerful, soaring vocals and multi-layered, distorted guitars. The production is polished and massive, creating a wall of sound where each element, especially the lead vocals and searing solos, still maintains clarity. A defining quality is the contrast between the heavy, rhythmic riffing and the highly technical, neoclassical guitar breaks, which establishes the track's epic scope from the beginning.

review.review_text: PCBender has always known how to build a song around an idea that earns its runtime, and 'Desire' does exactly that, opening with a thesis that cuts right to the bone: 'It's the wanting we desire, not the thing.' At a measured 83 BPM in A# minor, the track gives that lyric room to breathe, the guitars carrying a blues-rock weight that keeps the emotional lift grounded rather than sentimental. The chorus lands with real conviction, framing desire not as craving but as a compass, and by the time the bridge arrives with 'Let every wish that wakes me be the one I claim,' you feel the song has actually earned that turn. A highlight in his catalog.
anchors_used: lyric quote: 'It's the wanting we desire, not the thing'
lyric quote: 'Let every wish that wakes me be the one I claim'
